title: Joby Rolls Third Aircraft Off Production Line, Reports Q2 2024 Financial Results
text: Joby Aviation reported Q2 2024 financial results with strong operational progress. The company reported a net loss of $123 million but ended the quarter with a robust $825 million in cash and short-term investments. The Q2 net loss reflected a loss from operations of about $144 million, partly offset by interest and other income of $21 million. The company remained on track with its full year 2024 cash spending outlook of $440 to $470 million. Joby Aviation was 37 percent through the stage four certification process with the FAA and was actively working towards a type certificate by late 2025. The company rolled its third production prototype aircraft off the company's pilot production line in Marina, California, with four aircraft expected to be in active flight test during the next quarter. Joby was making strides in international expansion, targeting a commercial launch in Dubai next year. The company had established partnerships with companies like Uber and Delta to support demand generation and infrastructure development. The company flew a first-of-its-kind hydrogen-electric air taxi demonstrator 561 miles and acquired the autonomy division of Xwing, an industry leader in autonomous technology for aviation.
